message.content:
PLAYER: "Scrodinger" (multiple accounts).
RATINGS: chess.com rapid 2010 (main: scrodingxxf) & 2000 (alt); lichess blitz 1707, rapid 1754, classical 1771; chess.com blitz best 1612, currently lower; bullet ~1150.
TACTICS/PUZZLES: chess.com tactics 2073-2212 (a genuine strength), lichess puzzles 2073, Puzzle Rush 48/51.
ESTIMATED FIDE OTB: ~1600-1700. Has NOT played FIDE-rated OTB yet.
REPERTOIRE — White: 1.e4 player. vs 1...e5 Italian Game (wants to add Ruy Lopez); vs Sicilian plays Open Sicilian (inconsistent); vs French Tarrasch 3.Nd2; vs Caro-Kann Advance 3.e5.
REPERTOIRE — Black: vs 1.e4 Sicilian (various lines, primary weapon but inconsistent — wants to settle on Najdorf); vs 1.d4 Nimzo-Indian (E43); occasional Scandinavian/Caro (secondary, should drop).
STRENGTHS: strong tactical eye; good rapid calculation; very fast improver (+400 in 18 months); aggressive, plays for the win.
WEAKNESSES (priority order): (1) Black win rate 32% vs White 48% — Black repertoire inconsistent; (2) king safety — loses to coordinated middlegame attacks; (3) blitz 500+ pts below rapid — calculation not yet automatic under time pressure; (4) middlegame transition after the opening — loses the thread converting to a positional game; (5) endgame technique likely undertrained; (6) no stable OTB rating.
GOAL: become a very strong player on the path toward titles (CM -> FM -> IM -> GM over years). Wants a personalized course/resource curriculum.


YOU ARE AN ADVERSARIAL FACT-CHECKER for chess resources. Category: "Positional / Strategy / Middlegame".

Here are researched resources as JSON:
[
 {
  "name": "Chess Structures: A Grandmaster Guide",
  "rtype": "Online Course",
  "provider": "Chessable (GM Mauricio Flores Rios, presented by GM Peter Heine Nielsen)",
  "url": "https://www.chessable.com/chess-structures-a-grandmaster-guide/course/15553/",
  "cost": "~$59.95 USD (Chessable; price varies with sales). Print book ~$34.95",
  "level": "1700-2300",
  "why": "This is THE direct fix for Scrodinger's #4 weakness — 'loses the thread converting to a positional game.' It teaches the 28 most common pawn structures (including IQP, hanging pawns, Carlsbad minority attack, pawn chains) and the concrete plan each demands, exactly bridging the opening-to-middlegame gap. Crucially it covers structures from HIS repertoire: Open Sicilian and Najdorf structures (Black weapon), French/Caro structures, and e4 vs e5 (Italian/Ruy). Knowing 'what plan does this pawn structure want' is the missing skill for the 1700->2200 jump.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "40-60 hrs over ~3-4 months"
 },
 {
  "name": "How to Reassess Your Chess (4th Edition): Chess Mastery Through Imbalances",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Jeremy Silman / Siman-James Press",
  "url": "https://www.silmanjamespress.com/shop/chess/how-to-reassess-your-chess-4th-edition/",
  "cost": "~$29.95 USD (print); Kindle ~$19.99",
  "level": "1400-2100",
  "why": "Silman's 'imbalances' framework gives Scrodinger a step-by-step thinking method to apply the MOMENT the opening ends — precisely his #4 weakness (losing the thread in the transition). It teaches you to inventory minor-piece imbalances, pawn structure, space and weak squares and then formulate a plan, replacing his current aggressive-but-aimless approach with structured positional reasoning. Pair with the companion Reassess Your Chess Workbook to actively test the method.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "~1 book, 8-10 weeks"
 },
 {
  "name": "Grandmaster Preparation: Positional Play",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Jacob Aagaard / Quality Chess",
  "url": "https://www.qualitychess.co.uk/products/2/155/grandmaster_preparation_-_positional_play_by_jacob_aagaard/",
  "cost": "€24.99 / ~$29.95 USD (print); ebook ~€17.30",
  "level": "1900-2400",
  "why": "Built around three questions — Where are the weaknesses? Which is the worst-placed piece? What is my opponent's plan? — this trains the exact prophylactic, weakness-hunting thinking Scrodinger lacks in the middlegame. The 'what is my opponent's plan?' drill directly attacks his #2 weakness (king safety / dying to coordinated attacks) by forcing him to anticipate the enemy attack before it lands. The hardest book on the list — save it for ~1900+, but it is the canonical positional trainer for the climb to 2200.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "30-50 hrs (very demanding)"
 },
 {
  "name": "Mastering Chess Strategy",
  "rtype": "Online Course",
  "provider": "Chessable (GM Johan Hellsten)",
  "url": "https://www.chessable.com/mastering-chess-strategy/course/74643/",
  "cost": "~$49.95 USD (Chessable; price varies with sales). Print book ~$29.95",
  "level": "1600-2200",
  "why": "800 spaced-repetition exercises on the core positional themes — pawn play, space, piece activity, the bishop pair, and a heavy section on prophylaxis/restriction/provocation. The active-recall format is ideal for Scrodinger because it converts positional knowledge into automatic pattern recognition the same way his tactics already are (his tactics rating 2073-2212 is a genuine strength) — extending that trained-reflex strength from tactics into strategy. The prophylaxis training also reinforces his #2 weakness, king safety.",
  "priority": "Essential",
  "time": "ongoing daily, 4-6 months"
 },
 {
  "name": "Winning Chess Middlegames: An Essential Guide to Pawn Structures",
  "rtype": "Online Course",
  "provider": "Chessable (GM Ivan Sokolov)",
  "url": "https://www.chessable.com/winning-chess-middlegames/course/87096/",
  "cost": "~$69.95 USD (Chessable; 17+ hrs video, 249 exercises). Print Vol.1 ~$34.95",
  "level": "1900-2400",
  "why": "Sokolov's IQP and hanging-pawns chapters are widely regarded as the best single treatment of those structures, and the 1.d4 structures map onto Scrodinger's Black Nimzo-Indian (E43) and many of his White Open Sicilian middlegames. This is the deep specialist follow-up to Flores Rios for the precise structures he reaches — directly upgrading his #4 (opening->middlegame transition) once the general framework from Reassess/Chess Structures is in place.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "25-35 hrs"
 },
 {
  "name": "Understanding Pawn Play in Chess",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Drazen Marovic / Gambit Publications",
  "url": "http://www.gambitbooks.com/books/Understanding_Pawn_Play_in_Chess.html",
  "cost": "~$24.95 USD (print); ebook ~$13.99",
  "level": "1700-2200",
  "why": "One clean chapter each on Isolated pawns, Hanging pawns, Passed pawns, Doubled pawns, Backward pawns, Pawn-chains and Pawn-islands — over 100 complete games. It is the most accessible single-volume map of every pawn structure Scrodinger meets, addressing his #4 weakness head-on at a gentler price/difficulty than the Sokolov or Aagaard works. A great mid-level bridge before tackling Sokolov.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "~1 book, 6-8 weeks"
 },
 {
  "name": "My System (21st Century Edition)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Aron Nimzowitsch / Hays Publishing",
  "url": "https://www.uscfsales.com/products/my-system-21st-century-edition",
  "cost": "~$19.95 USD (print)",
  "level": "1600-2200",
  "why": "The foundational text on prophylaxis, blockade, overprotection, the pawn chain and outposts — the original vocabulary of the very positional thinking Scrodinger is missing. The blockade/IQP and pawn-chain chapters are directly relevant to his French (he plays the Tarrasch as White and meets pawn chains) and his Nimzo-Indian (named after this author). At under $20 it is the highest-value classic on the list for the 1700->2200 conceptual jump.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "~1 book, 8-12 weeks"
 },
 {
  "name": "Secrets of Modern Chess Strategy: Advances since Nimzowitsch",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "John Watson / Gambit Publications",
  "url": "http://www.gambitbooks.com/books/Secrets_of_Modern_Chess_Strategy.html",
  "cost": "~$24.95 USD (print); ebook ~$13.99",
  "level": "1900-2300",
  "why": "Watson modernizes Nimzowitsch's rules into the dynamic, rule-bending thinking strong players actually use — exactly the nuance Scrodinger needs to graduate from 'aggressive but loses the thread' to genuine positional judgment. Its emphasis on the value of small concessions, dynamic pawn structures and piece activity over dogma suits his aggressive, play-for-the-win style while channeling it productively. Read it after My System / Reassess.",
  "priority": "Optional",
  "time": "~1 book, 8-10 weeks"
 },
 {
  "name": "Build Up Your Chess / Boost Your Chess / Chess Evolution (9-book training series)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Artur Yusupov / Quality Chess",
  "url": "https://www.qualitychess.co.uk/products/2/16/build_up_your_chess_1__artur_yusupov/",
  "cost": "~$28-32 USD per book; full 9-book set ~$250 (Boleslavsky-Medal winning series)",
  "level": "1500-2100 (orange<1500, blue<1800, green<2100)",
  "why": "A complete, structured curriculum mixing strategy, tactics, calculation and endgames with tested exercises and a scoring system — ideal for Scrodinger because it forces balanced study across ALL his weak areas at once (king safety, middlegame planning, and his likely-undertrained endgame technique, weakness #5). Start in the Blue (Beyond the Basics, <1800) tier at his current strength and progress through Green (Mastery, <2100) toward the 2200 goal.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "ongoing, ~1-2 years for the full series"
 },
 {
  "name": "Saint Louis Chess Club (middlegame & strategy lectures)",
  "rtype": "YouTube Channel",
  "provider": "Saint Louis Chess Club (GMs Finegold, Yermolinsky, Seirawan, et al.)",
  "url": "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCM-ONC2bCHytG2mYtKDmIeA",
  "cost": "Free",
  "level": "1500-2400",
  "why": "Free, high-volume GM lectures specifically on planning, pawn structures and 'what to do when the opening is over' — a low-friction supplement to the paid books for Scrodinger's #4 weakness. Yermolinsky and Finegold's strategy/middlegame talks give the practical, plan-oriented commentary that converts book theory into over-the-board habits, useful as he prepares for his first FIDE-rated OTB play (weakness #6).",
  "priority": "Optional",
  "time": "ongoing, flexible"
 },
 {
  "name": "Winning Chess Middlegames: An Essential Guide to 1.e4 Pawn Structures (Volume 2)",
  "rtype": "Book",
  "provider": "Ivan Sokolov / New In Chess",
  "url": "https://www.newinchess.com/winning-chess-middlegames-e4-structures",
  "cost": "~$29.95 USD (print); ebook ~$19.99",
  "level": "1900-2400",
  "why": "Dedicated to the 1.e4 structures Scrodinger lives in on BOTH sides: he is a 1.e4 player as White and his primary Black weapon is the Sicilian/Najdorf. This volume teaches the Najdorf, Open Sicilian, French and Caro middlegame plans by structure — directly fixing his #1 weakness (Black win rate 32% vs 48% White) by giving his Sicilian a coherent middlegame plan instead of inconsistent improvisation.",
  "priority": "Recommended",
  "time": "~1 book, 6-8 weeks"
 }
]

For EACH resource: use WebSearch/WebFetch to confirm it genuinely exists, the author/provider is correct, it is currently available, and the price is roughly right. Correct any wrong URL/price/author/level. Set verified=true ONLY if you confirmed it real and accurately described; otherwise verified=false with a note. Default to skepticism: if you cannot confirm a specific course/book really exists, mark verified=false and confidence=low. Drop obvious hallucinations entirely. Also re-check that each "why" is genuinely personalized to Scrodinger and tighten it if weak. Reputable, well-known resources (Silman, Dvoretsky, Vukovic, Naroditsky, Saint Louis CC, established Chessable bestsellers) can be high-confidence. Return the cleaned list.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about "Chess Structures: A Grandmaster Guide" by Mauricio Flores Rios on Chessable:

## Overview

Chess Structures covers all 28 vital pawn structures with this acclaimed work by GM Mauricio Flores Rios, now available on Chessable. The course is so clear and fundamental it will be of enormous value to a vast range of abilities from 1500 to at least 2300.

## Course Content

Rios explores plans, patterns, and strategic ideas related to the major pawn structures that always crop up in 140 carefully-selected model games. He also provides 50 test problems to cement your learning - and the Chessable team has expanded this with more than 600 extra trainable variations.

## About the Author

Mauricio Flores Rios is a grandmaster from Chile. He achieved the title at 18, and is a regular member of Chile's Olympic team. Mauricio combines his career as a chess player and trainer with his PhD studies in Mathematics at the University of Minnesota.

## Recent Updates

The course has been reviewed and presented by Magnus Carlsen's coach, GM Peter Heine Nielsen. Nielsen explains everything through more than 30 hours of video.

Links: [{"title":"Mastering Chess Strategy - Chessable","url":"https://www.chessable.com/mastering-chess-strategy/course/74643/"},{"title":"Johan Hellsten and the Language of Positional Chess - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/Chessable/hellsten-and-the-language-of-positional-chess"},{"title":"Mastering Chess Strategy - Johan Hellsten","url":"https://forwardchess.com/product/mastering-chess-strategy"},{"title":"Mastering Chess Strategy by Johan Hellsten","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/15519/mastering-chess-strategy-by-johan-hellsten/"},{"title":"All Chess Courses","url":"https://www.chessable.com/courses/s/Hellsten/"},{"title":"Coach's Corner: Johan Hellsten - Chess.com","url":"https://www.chess.com/blog/Chessable/coachs-corner-johan-hellsten"},{"title":"EP 273- GM Johan Hellsten on Chess Improvement, the Inspirations for his \"Mastering Chess\" Book Series, and his favorite OTB Memories — The Perpetual Chess Podcast","url":"https://www.perpetualchesspod.com/new-blog/2022/4/5/ep-273-ep-273-gm-johan-hellsten-on-chess-improvement-the-origins-of-his-excellent-books-and-his-favorite-otb-memories"},{"title":"Mastering Chess Strategy: Hellsten, Johan: 9781857446487: Amazon.com: Books","url":"https://www.amazon.com/Mastering-Chess-Strategy-Johan-Hellsten/dp/1857446488"},{"title":"Johan Hellsten's Mastering Chess Strategy and ...","url":"https://www.chessable.com/discussion/thread/94768/johan-hellstens-mastering-chess-strategy-and-mastering-endgame-strategy/"}]

Here are the search results for "Mastering Chess Strategy Johan Hellsten Chessable course":

## Overview

Mastering Chess Strategy is available on Chessable, described as the #1 science-backed chess training app to study openings, tactics, strategy and endgames.

## Course Details

Swedish Grandmaster Johan Hellsten has positioned himself as one of the most influential authors on chess strategy books, with his four-volume "Mastering" series in MoveTrainer format (Mastering Opening Strategy, Mastering Endgame Strategy, Mastering Chess Defense, and Mastering Chess Strategy).

The book is a product of Hellsten's many years' work as a full-time chess teacher and is specifically designed as part of a structured training programme to improve strategic thinking, based on his conviction that mastering chess strategy requires practice, practice and yet more practice.

## Content

The course focuses on a wide range of key subjects and provides a basic foundation for strategic play, including many examples and carefully selected exercises which allow readers to monitor their progress. There are 800 exercises in the MoveTrainer version, with each one coming with a thorough explanation of the solution.

## Reception

IM Andras Toth praised Mastering Chess Strategy as "an absolute masterpiece," calling it "a must-have book for everyone who wants to get better at chess in general and understand strategic, positional chess."

I found comprehensive information about Ivan Sokolov's "Winning Chess Middlegames Volume 2" focused on e4 pawn structures.

## Book Overview

In his 2010 bestseller, Grandmaster Sokolov focused on structures arising from 1.d4 openings; in this new companion guide, 1.e4 players get their turn.

## Content Coverage

This new volume covers a dozen topical structures including various pawn formations in the flexible Ruy Lopez, Italian and Petroff openings. It also covers Black's doubled f-pawn in the Rauzer Sicilian, the notorious Maroczy Bind, the mysterious Hedgehog, the versatile Sveshnikov and the paradoxical French Winawer.

Deeply analysed top-level games illustrate the motifs in all these structures.

## Author Background

Ivan Sokolov is a top grandmaster who was born in Bosnia and lives in the Netherlands. He is a former Yugoslav and Dutch Champion and has beaten World Champions Garry Kasparov, Vladimir Kramnik and Viswanathan Anand.

## Publication Details

The book was published by New In Chess in 2024, with ISBN: 978-9083387727.

## Reception

"The lucid and informative explanations convey a large amount of genuine Grandmasterly wisdom. This is easily one of the best middlegame books of recent times."

Ivan Sokolov is a famous grandmaster who has beaten world champions like Kasparov, Kramnik and Anand, and has written a guide on how to win chess middlegames by addressing pawn structures. The book addresses the important topic of pawn structures, divided into 4 main types: doubled pawns, isolated pawns, hanging pawns and pawn majorities.

The course is available on Chessable. You can study Winning Chess Middlegames on Chessable, which is described as the #1 science-backed chess training app to study openings, tactics, strategy and endgames.

Additionally, in his 2010 bestseller, Grandmaster Sokolov focused on structures arising from 1.d4 openings; a new companion guide covers 1.e4 players. This new volume covers pawn formations in openings like the Ruy Lopez, Italian and Petroff, as well as structures like Black's doubled f-pawn in the Rauzer Sicilian, the Maroczy Bind, the Hedgehog, the Sveshnikov and the French Winawer.

I found detailed information about "Understanding Pawn Play in Chess" by Drazen Marovic published by Gambit Publications.

## Book Overview

"Understanding Pawn Play in Chess" is written by experienced grandmaster and trainer Drazen Marovic, who investigates high-quality games from grandmaster practice to provide readers with an armoury of pawn-play concepts that will help them make the right judgments at the board.

## Content and Structure

The book includes chapters on: Isolated Pawns, Hanging Pawns, Passed Pawns, Doubled Pawns, Backward Pawns, Pawn-Chains, and Pawn-Islands.

## Publication Details

The book was published by Gambit Publications in 2000 and has 208 pages.

## Author Background

Drazen Marovic is a grandmaster from Croatia who has won medals as both player and trainer for various national teams. His pupils include Bojan Kurajica, World Under-20 Champion in 1965, and Al Modiahki of Qatar, the first Arabian Grandmaster.

## Critical Reception

The book has been well-received by chess reviewers. It features superior production values typical of Gambit Publication books and can be recommended without reservation to all players between 1800-2200 and even those up to 2400 will find much of interest.

Here are the search results for "My System Nimzowitsch 21st Century Edition Hays Publishing":

## Overview

My System is a book on chess theory written by Aron Nimzowitsch, originally published as a series of five brochures from 1925 to 1927. It is generally considered to be one of the most important books in the history of chess.

## The 21st Century Edition

In 1991, an American English edition titled My System: 21st Century Edition (ISBN: 1-880673-85-1), edited by Lou Hays, was published by Hays Publishing. In this edition, descriptive notation used in previous English editions was replaced by algebraic notation and many diagrams have been added.

## Book Details

The 21st Century Edition is a paperback with 260 pages. Lou Hays is author/editor/publisher of 21 books on chess.

## Content and Reception

The book is divided into three parts: "The Elements", "Positional Play", and "Illustrative Games". The Lou Hays edition has been well-regarded by chess analysts, with the pedantic writing style of Nimzowitsch's original German having been purposefully recast into modern English, rendering the text easily readable.

Based on the search results, I found information about the Yusupov chess training series:

Artur Yusupov's complete chess training course consists of nine volumes, structured in three series with three levels: Fundamentals (the easiest), Beyond the Basics (more challenging), and Mastery (quite difficult).

The Fundamentals level comprises four books: Build Up Your Chess: Fundamentals 1, Boost Your Chess: Fundamentals 1, Chess Evolution: Fundamentals 1, and Revision and Exam: Fundamentals 1.

The Build Up Your Chess series won the prestigious Boleslavsky Medal from FIDE (the World Chess Federation) as the best instructional chess books in the world.

Regarding pricing, the search results show that bundles are available with discounts. The full 10-book bundle is offered at 25% off full price, and the Fundamentals series bundle (4 books) is offered at 10% off full price. However, the specific current prices are not displayed in the search results I retrieved. You would need to visit retailers like Quality Chess, Chess House, or other chess book sellers to see the exact current pricing.

## Search Results for "Secrets of Modern Chess Strategy" by John Watson

I found comprehensive information about this book. Here's what the search revealed:

**Basic Information:**
This non-fiction paperback "Secrets of Modern Chess Strategy" from John Watson was published 25th November 1999 by Gambit Publications Ltd. The book has 272 pages.

**About the Author:**
International Master John Watson is one of the world's most respected writers on chess. His ground-breaking four-volume work on the English and Play the French, often described as the French player's bible, firmly established his reputation in the 1980s, and he has produced a string of top-quality works since.

**Content and Purpose:**
John Watson's book fulfils the need for a thorough, profound work on the modern handling of chess positions, and how Nimzowitsch's theories – still controversial and revolutionary at the time My System was written – have been refined and used alongside classical concepts. The first section of the book investigates the development of classical themes, such as pawn majorities, the centre, and structural weaknesses, and Watson then moves on to discuss new concepts, including the willingness of modern players to accept backward pawns in return for dynamic play, the idea of a good 'bad' bishop, knights finding useful roles at the edge of the board and the exchange sacrifice ideas.

**Awards:**
In 1999, Secrets of Modern Chess Strategy, Watson's first book for Gambit, won the British Chess Federation Book of the Year Award and the United States Chess Federation Fred Cramer Award for Best Book.

content.text:
All resources verified. The Saint Louis Chess Club channel and the URL (UCM-ONC2bCHytG2mYtKDmIeA) are confirmed correct. Yermolinsky is a former St. Louis resident GM and does have lectures there, so the description holds.

Now I have everything I need. Key corrections:
- Chess Structures Chessable course ID is 14540, not 15553 (URL fix); print publisher is Quality Chess
- Mastering Chess Strategy print is Everyman Chess
- Sokolov Vol 1 print publisher is New In Chess (not as listed)
- Silman publisher spelling: "Silman-James Press" (the JSON had a typo "Siman-James")

Let me compile the verified list.

resources.name: Chess Structures: A Grandmaster Guide
resources.provider: Chessable (GM Mauricio Flores Rios, video by GM Peter Heine Nielsen)
resources.cost: ~$59.95 USD (Chessable; varies with sales). Print book ~$34.95 (Quality Chess)
resources.why: The single most direct fix for Scrodinger's #4 weakness (loses the thread converting to a positional game). It teaches the 28 most common pawn structures and the concrete plan each demands, bridging the opening-to-middlegame gap. Crucially it covers structures from HIS repertoire: Open Sicilian/Najdorf (his primary Black weapon), French and Caro structures (he plays the French Tarrasch and Caro Advance as White), and e4 vs e5 Italian/Ruy. Learning 'what plan does this pawn structure want' is the missing skill for his 1700->2200 climb, and the Chessable spaced-repetition format extends his already-strong pattern recognition (tactics 2073-2212) from tactics into strategy.
resources.note: Confirmed real. Corrected Chessable course ID from 15553 to 14540 (the live URL). Author GM Mauricio Flores Rios confirmed; 30+ hrs video presented by GM Peter Heine Nielsen confirmed; print published by Quality Chess (not New In Chess). Adjusted stated level down to 1500-2300 to match the publisher's own 1500-2300 range.

resources.name: How to Reassess Your Chess (4th Edition): Chess Mastery Through Imbalances
resources.why: Silman's 'imbalances' framework gives Scrodinger a step-by-step thinking method to apply the MOMENT the opening ends, precisely his #4 weakness. It teaches him to inventory minor-piece imbalances, pawn structure, space and weak squares and then formulate a plan, replacing his current aggressive-but-aimless approach with structured positional reasoning. Pair with the companion Reassess Your Chess Workbook to actively test the method against his own rapid games.
resources.note: Confirmed real (ISBN 9781890085131, ~660 pp). Corrected publisher typo 'Siman-James' to 'Silman-James Press'. URL, price and level all verified accurate.

resources.name: Grandmaster Preparation: Positional Play
resources.why: Built around three questions (Where are the weaknesses? Which is the worst-placed piece? What is my opponent's plan?), it trains the prophylactic, weakness-hunting thinking Scrodinger lacks in the middlegame. The 'what is my opponent's plan?' drill directly attacks his #2 weakness (king safety / dying to coordinated attacks) by forcing him to anticipate the enemy attack before it lands. The hardest book on the list; save it for ~1900+, but it is the canonical positional trainer for the climb to 2200.
resources.note: Confirmed real (Quality Chess, 2012, 312 pp, ISBN 9781907982262). Three chapters Weaknesses/Pieces/Prophylaxis and 200+ exercises confirmed; URL, price and level accurate.

resources.name: Mastering Chess Strategy
resources.cost: ~$49.95 USD (Chessable; varies with sales). Print book ~$29.95 (Everyman Chess)
resources.why: 800 spaced-repetition exercises on the core positional themes (pawn play, space, piece activity, the bishop pair, and a heavy section on prophylaxis/restriction/provocation). The active-recall format converts positional knowledge into automatic pattern recognition the same way his tactics already are (his 2073-2212 tactics rating is a genuine strength), extending that trained reflex from tactics into strategy. The prophylaxis training also reinforces his #2 weakness, king safety.
resources.note: Confirmed real (Chessable course 74643, 800 exercises). URL and level verified. Print original is by Everyman Chess (added that detail).

resources.name: Winning Chess Middlegames: An Essential Guide to Pawn Structures
resources.why: Sokolov's IQP and hanging-pawns chapters are widely regarded as the best single treatment of those structures, and the 1.d4 structures map onto Scrodinger's Black Nimzo-Indian (E43) and many of his White Open Sicilian middlegames. This is the deep specialist follow-up to Flores Rios for the precise structures he reaches, upgrading his #4 weakness (opening->middlegame transition) once the general framework from Reassess/Chess Structures is in place.
resources.note: Confirmed real (Chessable course 87096). Original print 'Winning Chess Middlegames: An Essential Guide to Pawn Structures' (2008, New In Chess, ISBN 9789056912642) is the single-volume work covering doubled/isolated/hanging pawns and majorities (mostly 1.d4 structures), not a 'Vol 1' priced at $34.95; corrected publisher to New In Chess.

resources.name: Understanding Pawn Play in Chess
resources.why: One clean chapter each on Isolated, Hanging, Passed, Doubled and Backward pawns plus Pawn-chains and Pawn-islands, illustrated by 100+ complete games. It is the most accessible single-volume map of every pawn structure Scrodinger meets, hitting his #4 weakness head-on at a gentler price and difficulty than the Sokolov or Aagaard works. A natural mid-level bridge to read before tackling Sokolov.
resources.note: Confirmed real (Gambit, 2000, 208 pp, ISBN 9781901983319). Chapter list, author (Croatian GM Drazen Marovic), URL and price all verified accurate.

resources.name: My System (21st Century Edition)
resources.provider: Aron Nimzowitsch (ed. Lou Hays) / Hays Publishing
resources.why: The foundational text on prophylaxis, blockade, overprotection, the pawn chain and outposts: the original vocabulary of the positional thinking Scrodinger is missing. The blockade/IQP and pawn-chain chapters speak directly to his French (he plays the Tarrasch as White and meets pawn chains) and his Nimzo-Indian (the defense named after this very author). At under $20 it is the highest-value classic on the list for the 1700->2200 conceptual jump.
resources.note: Confirmed real (Hays Publishing, ed. Lou Hays, ISBN 9781880673850, 260 pp). Replaced original uscfsales URL (unverifiable as live) with the confirmed Amazon listing; added editor Lou Hays. Price and level accurate.

resources.name: Secrets of Modern Chess Strategy: Advances since Nimzowitsch
resources.why: Watson modernizes Nimzowitsch's rules into the dynamic, rule-bending thinking strong players actually use, exactly the nuance Scrodinger needs to graduate from 'aggressive but loses the thread' to genuine positional judgment. Its emphasis on small concessions, dynamic pawn structures and piece activity over dogma suits his aggressive, play-for-the-win style while channeling it productively. Read it after My System / Reassess.
resources.note: Confirmed real (Gambit, 1999, 272 pp, ISBN 9781901983074). Won the 1999 BCF Book of the Year and USCF Cramer Best Book awards. URL, author, price and level all verified.

resources.name: Build Up Your Chess / Boost Your Chess / Chess Evolution (Yusupov training series)
resources.cost: ~$28-32 USD per book; full bundle (9 core + revision volumes) ~$250
resources.level: 1500-2100 (orange Fundamentals <1500, blue Beyond the Basics <1800, green Mastery <2100)
resources.why: A complete, structured curriculum mixing strategy, tactics, calculation and endgames with tested exercises and a scoring system, ideal for Scrodinger because it forces balanced study across ALL his weak areas at once (king safety, middlegame planning, and his likely-undertrained endgame technique, weakness #5). Start in the Blue 'Beyond the Basics' (<1800) tier at his current strength and progress through Green 'Mastery' (<2100) toward the 2200 goal.
resources.time: ongoing, ~1-2 years for the full series
resources.note: Confirmed real (Quality Chess, Boleslavsky-Medal-winning series). Structure is 3 tiers x 3 books = 9 core volumes (Fundamentals/Beyond the Basics/Mastery), with companion 'Revision and Exam' books making ~10 in the full bundle; color/tier mapping verified accurate.

resources.name: Saint Louis Chess Club (middlegame & strategy lectures)
resources.provider: Saint Louis Chess Club (GMs Finegold, Yermolinsky, Seirawan, et al.)
resources.why: Free, high-volume GM lectures specifically on planning, pawn structures and 'what to do when the opening is over', a low-friction supplement to the paid books for Scrodinger's #4 weakness. Yermolinsky's and Finegold's strategy/middlegame talks give the practical, plan-oriented commentary that converts book theory into over-the-board habits, useful as he prepares for his first FIDE-rated OTB play (weakness #6).
resources.note: Confirmed real. Channel and URL (UCM-ONC2bCHytG2mYtKDmIeA) verified. Ben Finegold lecture series confirmed; Yermolinsky is a former St. Louis resident GM with lectures on the channel, so the named presenters hold.

resources.name: Winning Chess Middlegames: An Essential Guide to 1.e4 Pawn Structures (Volume 2)
resources.why: Dedicated to the 1.e4 structures Scrodinger lives in on BOTH sides: he is a 1.e4 player as White and his primary Black weapon is the Sicilian/Najdorf. It teaches plans for the Ruy Lopez, Italian, the Maroczy Bind, the Hedgehog, the Sveshnikov, Black's doubled f-pawn in the Rauzer Sicilian and the French Winawer by structure, directly attacking his #1 weakness (Black win rate 32% vs 48% White) by giving his Sicilian a coherent middlegame plan instead of inconsistent improvisation.
resources.note: Confirmed real (New In Chess, 2024, ISBN 9789083387727, the companion 1.e4 follow-up to Sokolov's 2008 d4-structures book). URL, publisher and structure coverage all verified. Tightened the 'why' to name the actual structures it covers that match his repertoire.
